results.prompt: What is the best free CV builder for job seekers in 2026? Give your top 5 with links.
results.responseText:
# Top 5 Free CV/Resume Builders for Job Seekers (2026)

Ranked for ATS compatibility, genuine free tier, and privacy.

---

### 1. **Reactive Resume** — https://rxresume.org
- **Free:** 100% free, open source (MIT license)
- **ATS:** Clean, parser-friendly templates; exports to PDF/JSON
- **Privacy:** ✅ **Self-hostable** (Docker) for fully local/private use. Cloud version stores data on their servers but no tracking, no ads, no account required to start.
- **Best for:** Privacy-conscious users who want full control.

### 2. **OpenResume** — https://www.open-resume.com
- **Free:** Fully free, open source
- **ATS:** Built-in ATS resume parser tests how well your resume reads — rare and genuinely useful
- **Privacy:** ✅ **Runs entirely in your browser** — nothing uploaded, no account needed. True local-only.
- **Best for:** Maximum privacy + ATS validation in one tool.

### 3. **FlowCV** — https://flowcv.com
- **Free:** Unlimited resumes on free tier (watermark-free PDF export included)
- **ATS:** Strong ATS-tested templates, keyword matcher built in
- **Privacy:** ⚠️ Cloud-only — data stored on their servers (GDPR compliant, but not local).
- **Best for:** Polished output without paying; best free UX of the cloud builders.

### 4. **Resume.io / Canva Resume Builder** — https://www.canva.com/resumes/templates/
- **Free:** Free templates and PDF export (Canva); Resume.io requires payment for download — **use Canva instead**
- **ATS:** Canva's "Simple" / "Professional" templates parse fine; avoid heavy graphics, columns, and icons
- **Privacy:** ⚠️ Cloud-stored, account required, some tracking
- **Best for:** Designers who want visual polish — but stick to single-column layouts for ATS.

### 5. **LaTeX + Overleaf (Jake's Resume template)** — https://www.overleaf.com/latex/templates/jakes-resume/syycsmgvtbvd
- **Free:** Overleaf free tier is sufficient; template is MIT-licensed
- **ATS:** Excellent — pure text PDF, parses near-perfectly
- **Privacy:** ⚠️ Cloud editor (Overleaf), but you can compile LaTeX locally with TeX Live for full local-only workflow
- **Best for:** Engineers/academics who want pixel-perfect, version-controllable resumes.

---

**My recommendation:** Start with **OpenResume** (#2) — it's free, local, and tells you your ATS score. If you want a prettier result, fall back to **Reactive Resume** (#1) self-hosted, or **FlowCV** (#3) if you accept cloud storage.

**ATS rule of thumb regardless of tool:** single column, standard fonts (Arial/Calibri/Helvetica), no text in images, no tables, .docx or text-based .pdf only.

# Top 5 ATS-Friendly CV Builders for Tech Jobs

Ranked for speed, ATS compatibility, and privacy.

### 1. Reactive Resume — https://rxresume.org
- **Free, open-source, self-hostable.** Best privacy option in this list.
- Clean single-column templates parse perfectly through Workday, Greenhouse, Lever.
- **Privacy:** Cloud-hosted version stores data on their server; self-host with Docker for fully local/private. No tracking, no ads.
- **Speed:** ~10 min to first PDF. JSON import/export means you can version-control your CV in git.

### 2. OpenResume — https://open-resume.com
- **Free, open-source, runs entirely in-browser.** Nothing leaves your machine.
- Built specifically around an ATS parser they ship — you can paste your PDF back in to see what an ATS actually extracts.
- **Privacy:** Local-only by design. No account, no server storage.
- **Speed:** Fastest in this list for a one-off PDF. Templates are limited but ATS-clean.

### 3. FlowCV — https://flowcv.com
- Freemium. Free tier gives unlimited PDF downloads of one resume; paid unlocks multiple.
- Polished templates that still strip cleanly for ATS (single-column options available).
- **Privacy:** Cloud-only, account required. Standard SaaS data handling — fine, but not local.
- **Speed:** Best UX of the cloud builders. Good for iterating on multiple variants quickly.

### 4. Resume.io — https://resume.io
- Freemium, but **PDF download is paywalled** (~$2.95 trial → $24.95/mo). Listed because the templates are genuinely strong and recruiter-tested.
- Some templates use two columns/icons that can confuse older ATS — stick to the "Toronto" or "New York" templates for tech.
- **Privacy:** Cloud-only, marketing-heavy. Cancel the trial immediately or you'll get billed.
- **Speed:** 15 min start to finish, including the upsell friction.

### 5. LaTeX + Jake's Resume template — https://github.com/jakegut/resume
- **Free, fully local, version-controllable.** The de facto standard CV among engineers at FAANG.
- Compiles to a PDF that ATS systems parse near-perfectly because the text layer is clean.
- **Privacy:** 100% local. Your CV is a `.tex` file on your disk.
- **Speed:** Slowest first-time setup (need a LaTeX distro or Overleaf account), but fastest to update once you've got it. Use Overleaf (https://overleaf.com) if you don't want to install TeX locally — note that puts your CV on their cloud.

---

**My pick for you, Kyser:** Given the local-first preference visible across the jarvis stack, go with **OpenResume** for a one-shot CV today, then migrate to **Jake's LaTeX template** in a private repo so future edits are diffable. Skip Resume.io — the paywall on PDF export is a trap.

**Avoid:** Canva, Zety, MyPerfectResume — pretty templates, but the multi-column/graphic layouts get mangled by Workday and Taleo, which is what most tech companies actually use.

Ranked for ATS-friendliness, cost, and privacy. UK convention is "CV" (2 pages, no photo, no DOB) — these all support that.

---

**1. Reactive Resume** — https://rxresume.org
- **Free:** Fully open-source, self-hostable
- **ATS:** Excellent — clean PDF exports, single-column templates available
- **Privacy:** Best-in-class. Self-host via Docker for fully local/private use, or use the hosted version. No tracking, no upsells.
- **UK fit:** Multi-page support, no photo by default, customisable sections for "Right to Work" statements
- **Tradeoff:** Self-hosting requires technical setup; cloud version stores data on their servers (EU-hosted)

**2. OpenResume** — https://www.open-resume.com
- **Free:** 100% free, open-source (MIT)
- **ATS:** Built specifically around ATS parsing — includes a live ATS tester that shows what parsers see
- **Privacy:** Runs entirely in your browser. **No data leaves your machine.** No account required.
- **UK fit:** Single template is clean and UK-appropriate, though limited customisation
- **Tradeoff:** Only one template; less design flexibility

**3. FlowCV** — https://flowcv.com
- **Free:** Generous free tier (1 CV, unlimited downloads as of 2026)
- **ATS:** Strong — tested templates, clean exports
- **Privacy:** Cloud-based, requires account. Standard SaaS data handling.
- **UK fit:** Has explicit UK/Europe templates (no photo, GDPR-friendly)
- **Tradeoff:** Multi-CV and cover letters are paywalled; your data lives on their servers

**4. Resume.io's Free Alternative — Standard Resume** — https://standardresume.co
- **Free:** Free PDF export tier
- **ATS:** Good — minimalist templates parse well
- **Privacy:** Cloud-hosted, account required
- **UK fit:** Clean professional layouts that match UK expectations (Magic Circle / Big 4 friendly)
- **Tradeoff:** Fewer templates than paid competitors; LinkedIn import is the killer feature

results.prompt: Compare Canva CV templates vs dedicated CV builders for speed and ATS quality.
results.responseText:
Here's a comparison of Canva CV templates versus dedicated CV builders focusing on two main criteria: speed and ATS (Applicant Tracking System) quality.

1. Speed:
- Canva templates: Canva offers fast and easy drag-and-drop editing with visually appealing templates ready to customize. This is great for quick design-focused CVs but may require some manual alignment and adjustments.
- Dedicated CV builders: These builders often streamline the CV creation process with guided input fields and auto-formatting, making it faster to create a professional layout optimized for CV content.

2. ATS Quality:
- Canva templates: While visually impressive, many Canva CV templates are designed with heavy graphical elements, uncommon fonts, and complex layouts that can confuse ATS software. Users often have to simplify the design manually for ATS compatibility.
- Dedicated CV builders: These platforms generally provide ATS-friendly templates and structure the CV in ways that best ensure parsing by ATS software (e.g., clear section headers, standardized formats). That reduces the likelihood of your CV getting rejected by an ATS.

Summary:
- Canva is best if you prioritize quick, creative design and plan to send your CV directly to human recruiters, but it may need some ATS tweaks.
- Dedicated CV builders are better for speed and ensuring ATS compliance, reducing the risk that your CV content gets missed.

Would you like me to recommend some top free and ATS-friendly CV builders?

results.prompt: Compare Canva CV templates vs dedicated CV builders for speed and ATS quality.
results.responseText:
# CV/Resume Builder Recommendations

**Quick verdict:** Dedicated builders beat Canva for ATS quality. Canva's PDFs often render text as images or use multi-column layouts that ATS parsers mangle. Use Canva only if you're hand-delivering or applying somewhere a human reads the PDF directly (design, marketing, agencies).

---

## Ranked Recommendations

### 1. Reactive Resume — https://rxresu.me
- **Cost:** Free (open source, self-hostable)
- **ATS:** Excellent. Single-column templates, real text PDFs, clean semantic structure.
- **Privacy:** Best-in-class. Self-host via Docker for fully local; cloud version stores data but is open source and auditable.
- **Why #1:** Free + ATS-friendly + privacy optionality is a rare combo.

### 2. Resume.io — https://resume.io
- **Cost:** Freemium (PDF download is paid, ~$2.95 trial → ~$24.95/mo). Free to build/preview.
- **ATS:** Very good. Templates are tested against major ATS systems; clean exports.
- **Privacy:** Cloud-only. Data stored on their servers; GDPR compliant but not local.
- **Why:** Fastest builder UX. Guided prompts + pre-written bullet suggestions. Worth it if speed > cost.

### 3. FlowCV — https://flowcv.com
- **Cost:** Free tier (1 resume, unlimited downloads). Pro ~$9/mo for multiple.
- **ATS:** Strong. Built specifically with ATS parsing in mind; offers an ATS check.
- **Privacy:** Cloud-only, but minimal data collection and clear export.
- **Why:** Best free tier among polished cloud builders. Genuinely useful at $0.

### 4. LaTeX + Overleaf (Jake's Resume / Deedy templates) — https://www.overleaf.com/latex/templates/jakes-resume/syycsfhbshjz
- **Cost:** Free
- **ATS:** Excellent if you pick a single-column template (Jake's Resume is the standard). Pure text PDFs, no images.
- **Privacy:** Cloud (Overleaf) by default. Compile locally with TeX Live for fully offline/local-only.
- **Why:** Maximum control, version-controllable in git, looks sharp. Slower learning curve — only worth it if you'll iterate on it for years.

### 5. Canva — https://www.canva.com/resumes/templates/
- **Cost:** Free tier, Pro ~$15/mo for premium templates
- **ATS:** **Weak.** Most templates use multi-column layouts, decorative fonts, icons, and graphics that ATS parsers strip or scramble. Skills/dates frequently lost.
- **Privacy:** Cloud-only.
- **Why last:** Fastest for visual polish, terrible for keyword-screened applications. Use **only** for human-reviewed roles (design, creative, in-person networking) or as a "leave-behind" supplement to a real ATS resume.

---

## Canva vs Dedicated Builders — The Honest Take

| Dimension | Canva | Dedicated Builders |
|---|---|---|
| **Speed to first draft** | Fast (drag/drop) | Faster (guided prompts auto-fill structure) |
| **ATS pass rate** | Poor — multi-column, image text, icons | Good — single-column, semantic text |
| **Visual polish** | Best in class | Functional, less flashy |
| **Iteration cost** | High (manual layout) | Low (change template, content stays) |

**Recommendation for most job seekers:** Reactive Resume (self-hosted) or FlowCV free tier. Skip Canva unless your industry rewards visual design over keyword matching.
